message:
feat(fcp-host): add HTTP-style conditional cache validation for discovery and introspection

Implement ETag/If-None-Match and Last-Modified/If-Modified-Since
conditional validation for the discovery and introspection endpoints,
enabling agents to skip full payload deserialization when content
has not changed.

discovery.rs:
- Add CacheMetadata with strong ETag (blake3 of serialized payload),
  last_modified timestamp, max_age_seconds, and optional
  stale-while-revalidate budget
- Add CacheValidator with if_none_match and if_modified_since fields,
  and is_not_modified() that checks ETag equality first, then falls
  back to timestamp comparison
- Add ResponseMeta carrying application-level status code and message
  (304 Not Modified, etc.)
- Extend DiscoveryResponse and IntrospectionResponse with optional
  cache and meta fields
- Add discover_query() accepting optional CacheValidator, returning
  304 Not Modified with cache metadata when content matches
- Add introspect_with_cache() with same conditional validation for
  single-connector introspection
- Add discovery_cache_metadata() and introspection_cache_metadata()
  helpers that produce deterministic ETags from sorted/canonical
  payload representations
- Extend DiscoveryCache with last_modified tracking and
  cache_metadata() builder
- Add comprehensive tests for ETag generation, 304 responses,
  cache miss paths, and Last-Modified validation
- Add DiscoveryCacheResult.last_modified propagation through cache layer

fcp-host binary:
- Wire discover_query() and introspect_with_cache() into HTTP
  handler, extracting If-None-Match/If-Modified-Since from headers
- Add blake3 dependency to Cargo.toml

integration tests:
- Add conditional cache validation integration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
